tool_args.label: Queensboro Bridge Bus Priority Study
tool_args.raw_text: The Queensboro Bridge Bus Priority Study - Identify cost-effective ways to improve bus speed and reliability for local and express buses that use the QBB.

payload.description: Identify cost-effective ways to improve bus speed and reliability for local and express buses that use the QBB. Study principles: Consult stakeholders, Ensure pedestrian and driver safety, Balance the needs of all bridge users, Evaluate benefits and potential impacts, Minimize impact on adjacent neighborhoods.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Identify cost-effective ways to improve bus speed and reliability for local and express buses that use the QBB.
Consult stakeholders • Ensure pedestrian and driver safety • Balance the needs of all bridge users • Evaluate benefits and potential impacts • Minimize impact on adjacent neighborhoods

Queens side includes: - Queens Plaza - Ramp connections to 21 st St./Thomson Ave. - Thomson Ave - Queens Blvd - Northern Blvd • Manhattan Side includes area from Fifth Ave to the East River from 57 th Street to 63 rd Street

tool_args.label: Queensboro Bridge (QBB)
tool_args.raw_text: The Queensboro Bridge (QBB) is a major bus corridor – 16,000 bus trips a day

payload.description: Major bus corridor connecting Queens and Manhattan. Also known as QBB. 16,000 bus trips a day.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Queensboro Bridge (QBB) is a major bus corridor – 16,000 bus trips a day, including about 8,000 local riders – 110 buses during PM rush hour or one bus every 30 seconds
